title: quercus singuliflora
intro: Quercus singuliflora is a species of deciduous tree native to East Asia, specifically in China, characterized by its distinctive single-flowered inflorescences. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus singuliflora include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems, while also showing promise in protecting against cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. In traditional Chinese medicine, the bark is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal uses, and it may also be processed into extracts or tablets for easier consumption.
